heading: Professional archivist; Secretary’s authority to obtain services from General Services Administration
label: 6541 Professional archivist; Secretary’s authority to obtain services from General Services Administration
text: For each fiscal year (beginning with the fiscal year which ends ), the Secretary of the Senate is authorized to expend from the contingent fund of the Senate such amount as may be necessary to enable the Secretary to obtain from the General Services Administration the services of a professional archivist. Such services shall be obtained on a reimbursable basis and shall not be obtained except with the consent of the General Services Administration and the Committee on Rules and Administration.September 30, 1982
notes: CodificationSection was formerly classified to prior to editorial reclassification and renumbering as this section.section 61b–3 of this title

Reimbursement of Archivist of the United States for Expenditures for Project To Provide for Preservation of Records of Continuing Value of Senate; Payment, etc., of Amounts, , , provided that for the fiscal year ending , and for each of the next three succeeding fiscal years, the Secretary of the Senate was authorized to pay to the General Services Administration such amounts, not to exceed $300,000, needed to reimburse the Archivist of the United States for certain expenditures made to conduct a project to provide for the proper preservation of the Senate’s records of continuing value.Pub. L. 97–257, title I, § 10796 Stat. 850Sept. 10, 1982Sept. 30, 1982
